_note: custom_code NETWORKED example — verified live 2026-07-17f. The sandbox has NO fetch/axios/require; HTTP goes through the `customRequest` object (keys: get,post,put,delete,patch,head,options). Its signature is NOT axios-style: customRequest.get(url,{headers}) is normal, but customRequest.put(url, body, {headers}) SILENTLY DROPS the config → 401 'version header was not found' (looks like auth, is really signature). Correct: customRequest.put(url, {headers, data}). Read the body as (r && r.data) ? r.data : r. custom_code runs on premium-actions-worker → it is a PREMIUM/billed action. `attributes.output` MUST be a hand-populated sample object or PUBLISH BLOCKS — it is a placeholder shape, not runtime data. This example targets the contact's latest-start appointment on a given calendar and confirms it (the house pattern for update_appointment_status when a contact can hold >1 appointment — see references/step-shapes.md). A PUT to /calendars/events/appointments/{id} DOES fire GHL's appointment-status triggers.
name: Confirm latest appointment (by calendar)
